verification_expression: (text-alternative-present AND text-alternative-serves-equivalent-purpose) OR name-describes-purpose OR text-alternative-provides-descriptive-identification OR (captcha-text-alternative-describes-purpose AND captcha-alternative-sensory-forms-provided) OR can-be-ignored-by-assistive-technology

postconditions.name: text-alternative-present
postconditions.evidence: All non-text content that is presented to the user has a text alternative that serves the equivalent purpose

postconditions.name: text-alternative-serves-equivalent-purpose
postconditions.evidence: All non-text content that is presented to the user has a text alternative that serves the equivalent purpose

postconditions.name: name-describes-purpose
postconditions.evidence: If non-text content is a control or accepts user input, then it has a name that describes its purpose.

postconditions.name: text-alternative-provides-descriptive-identification
postconditions.evidence: text alternatives at least provide descriptive identification of the non-text content

postconditions.name: captcha-text-alternative-describes-purpose
postconditions.evidence: text alternatives that identify and describe the purpose of the non-text content are provided

postconditions.name: captcha-alternative-sensory-forms-provided
postconditions.evidence: alternative forms of CAPTCHA using output modes for different types of sensory perception are provided to accommodate different disabilities

postconditions.name: can-be-ignored-by-assistive-technology
postconditions.evidence: it is implemented in a way that it can be ignored by assistive technology

postconditions.name: alternative-for-time-based-media-presents-equivalent-information
postconditions.evidence: An alternative for time-based media is provided that presents equivalent information for prerecorded audio-only content.

postconditions.name: audio-track-presents-equivalent-information
postconditions.evidence: Either an alternative for time-based media or an audio track is provided that presents equivalent information for prerecorded video-only content.

postconditions.name: captions-provided
postconditions.evidence: Captions are provided for all prerecorded audio content in synchronized media

postconditions.name: alternative-for-time-based-media-provided
postconditions.evidence: An alternative for time-based media or audio description of the prerecorded video content is provided for synchronized media

postconditions.name: audio-description-provided
postconditions.evidence: An alternative for time-based media or audio description of the prerecorded video content is provided for synchronized media

postconditions.name: captions-provided
postconditions.evidence: Captions are provided for all live audio content in synchronized media.

postconditions.name: audio-description-provided
postconditions.evidence: Audio description is provided for all prerecorded video content in synchronized media.

postconditions.name: sign-language-interpretation-provided
postconditions.evidence: Sign language interpretation is provided for all prerecorded audio content in synchronized media.

postconditions.name: extended-audio-description-provided
postconditions.evidence: extended audio description is provided for all prerecorded video content in synchronized media

postconditions.name: alternative-for-time-based-media-provided
postconditions.evidence: An alternative for time-based media is provided for all prerecorded synchronized media and for all prerecorded video-only media.

postconditions.name: alternative-for-time-based-media-presents-equivalent-information
postconditions.evidence: An alternative for time-based media that presents equivalent information for live audio-only content is provided.

postconditions.name: info-structure-relationships-programmatically-determinable
postconditions.evidence: Information, structure, and relationships conveyed through presentation can be programmatically determined

postconditions.name: info-structure-relationships-available-in-text
postconditions.evidence: Information, structure, and relationships conveyed through presentation can be programmatically determined or are available in text.

postconditions.name: correct-reading-sequence-programmatically-determinable
postconditions.evidence: a correct reading sequence can be programmatically determined

postconditions.name: instructions-understandable-without-relying-solely-on-sensory-characteristics
postconditions.evidence: Instructions provided for understanding and operating content do not rely solely on sensory characteristics of components such as shape, color, size, visual location, orientation, or sound.

postconditions.name: view-and-operation-not-restricted-to-single-display-orientation
postconditions.evidence: Content does not restrict its view and operation to a single display orientation, such as portrait or landscape, unless a specific display orientation is essential.

postconditions.name: input-purpose-programmatically-determinable
postconditions.evidence: The purpose of each input field collecting information about the user can be programmatically determined

postconditions.name: purpose-of-components-icons-regions-programmatically-determinable
postconditions.evidence: the purpose of user interface components, icons, and regions can be programmatically determined

postconditions.name: information-conveyed-by-means-other-than-color
postconditions.evidence: Color is not used as the only visual means of conveying information, indicating an action, prompting a response, or distinguishing a visual element.

postconditions.name: mechanism-to-pause-or-stop-audio-available
postconditions.evidence: either a mechanism is available to pause or stop the audio

postconditions.name: mechanism-to-control-audio-volume-independently-available
postconditions.evidence: a mechanism is available to control audio volume independently from the overall system volume level

postconditions.name: text-contrast-ratio-at-least-4.5-to-1
postconditions.evidence: The visual presentation of text and images of text has a contrast ratio of at least 4.5:1

postconditions.name: large-text-contrast-ratio-at-least-3-to-1
postconditions.evidence: Large-scale text and images of large-scale text have a contrast ratio of at least 3:1

postconditions.name: text-resizable-up-to-200-percent-without-assistive-technology
postconditions.evidence: text can be resized without assistive technology up to 200 percent

postconditions.name: no-loss-of-content-or-functionality
postconditions.evidence: up to 200 percent without loss of content or functionality

postconditions.name: information-conveyed-by-text-rather-than-images-of-text
postconditions.evidence: text is used to convey information rather than images of text

postconditions.name: image-of-text-visually-customizable
postconditions.evidence: The image of text can be visually customized to the user's requirements

postconditions.name: text-presentation-essential
postconditions.evidence: A particular presentation of text is essential to the information being conveyed

postconditions.name: text-contrast-ratio-at-least-7-to-1
postconditions.evidence: The visual presentation of text and images of text has a contrast ratio of at least 7:1

postconditions.name: large-text-contrast-ratio-at-least-4-5-to-1
postconditions.evidence: Large-scale text and images of large-scale text have a contrast ratio of at least 4.5:1

postconditions.name: no-background-sounds
postconditions.evidence: No Background The audio does not contain background sounds

postconditions.name: background-sounds-can-be-turned-off
postconditions.evidence: Turn Off The background sounds can be turned off

postconditions.name: background-sounds-at-least-20db-lower-than-foreground
postconditions.evidence: 20 dB The background sounds are at least 20 decibels lower than the foreground speech content

verification_expression: foreground-and-background-colors-user-selectable AND width-no-more-than-80-characters AND text-not-justified AND line-and-paragraph-spacing-sufficient AND text-resizable-to-200-percent-without-horizontal-scrolling

postconditions.name: foreground-and-background-colors-user-selectable
postconditions.evidence: Foreground and background colors can be selected by the user

postconditions.name: width-no-more-than-80-characters
postconditions.evidence: Width is no more than 80 characters or glyphs (40 if CJK)

postconditions.name: text-not-justified
postconditions.evidence: Text is not justified (aligned to both the left and the right margins)

postconditions.name: line-and-paragraph-spacing-sufficient
postconditions.evidence: Line spacing (leading) is at least space-and-a-half within paragraphs, and paragraph spacing is at least 1.5 times larger than the line spacing

postconditions.name: text-resizable-to-200-percent-without-horizontal-scrolling
postconditions.evidence: Text can be resized without assistive technology up to 200 percent in a way that does not require the user to scroll horizontally to read a line of text on a full-screen window

postconditions.name: image-of-text-used-only-for-pure-decoration
postconditions.evidence: Images of text are only used for pure decoration

postconditions.name: text-presentation-essential
postconditions.evidence: where a particular presentation of text is essential to the information being conveyed

postconditions.name: no-loss-of-information-or-functionality
postconditions.evidence: Content can be presented without loss of information or functionality

postconditions.name: no-scrolling-in-two-dimensions
postconditions.evidence: without requiring scrolling in two dimensions for: Vertical scrolling content at a width equivalent to 320 CSS pixels; Horizontal scrolling content at a height equivalent to 256 CSS pixels

postconditions.name: ui-component-visual-information-contrast-ratio-at-least-3-to-1
postconditions.evidence: User Interface Components Visual information required to identify user interface components and states ... have a contrast ratio of at least 3:1 against adjacent color(s)

postconditions.name: graphical-object-contrast-ratio-at-least-3-to-1
postconditions.evidence: Graphical Objects Parts of graphics required to understand the content ... have a contrast ratio of at least 3:1 against adjacent color(s)

postconditions.name: no-loss-of-content-or-functionality-when-text-spacing-properties-overridden
postconditions.evidence: no loss of content or functionality occurs by setting all of the following and by changing no other style property: Line height (line spacing) to at least 1.5 times the font size; Spacing following paragraphs to at least 2 times the font size; Letter spacing (tracking) to at least 0.12 times the font size; Word spacing to at least 0.16 times the font size

postconditions.name: additional-content-dismissible-without-moving-pointer-or-focus
postconditions.evidence: Dismissible A mechanism is available to dismiss the additional content without moving pointer hover or keyboard focus

postconditions.name: additional-content-hoverable-without-disappearing
postconditions.evidence: Hoverable If pointer hover can trigger the additional content, then the pointer can be moved over the additional content without the additional content disappearing

postconditions.name: additional-content-persistent-until-trigger-removed
postconditions.evidence: Persistent The additional content remains visible until the hover or focus trigger is removed, the user dismisses it, or its information is no longer valid

postconditions.name: all-functionality-operable-through-keyboard-interface
postconditions.evidence: All functionality of the content is operable through a keyboard interface

postconditions.name: no-specific-timing-required-for-individual-keystrokes
postconditions.evidence: without requiring specific timings for individual keystrokes

postconditions.name: focus-can-be-moved-away-using-keyboard
postconditions.evidence: then focus can be moved away from that component using only a keyboard interface

postconditions.name: standard-exit-methods-sufficient
postconditions.evidence: if it requires more than unmodified arrow or tab keys or other standard exit methods

postconditions.name: nonstandard-exit-method-advised-to-user
postconditions.evidence: the user is advised of the method for moving focus away

postconditions.name: all-functionality-keyboard-operable
postconditions.evidence: All functionality of the content is operable through a keyboard interface

postconditions.name: no-specific-keystroke-timing-required
postconditions.evidence: without requiring specific timings for individual keystrokes

postconditions.name: shortcut-can-be-turned-off
postconditions.evidence: Turn off A mechanism is available to turn the shortcut off

postconditions.name: shortcut-can-be-remapped-to-non-printable-keys
postconditions.evidence: Remap A mechanism is available to remap the shortcut to include one or more non-printable keyboard keys (e.g., Ctrl, Alt)

postconditions.name: shortcut-active-only-on-component-focus
postconditions.evidence: Active only on focus The keyboard shortcut for a user interface component is only active when that component has focus

verification_expression: time-limit-can-be-turned-off OR time-limit-can-be-adjusted OR time-limit-can-be-extended OR time-limit-required-for-real-time-event OR time-limit-essential-to-activity OR time-limit-longer-than-twenty-hours

postconditions.name: time-limit-can-be-turned-off
postconditions.evidence: Turn off The user is allowed to turn off the time limit before encountering it

postconditions.name: time-limit-can-be-adjusted
postconditions.evidence: Adjust The user is allowed to adjust the time limit before encountering it over a wide range that is at least ten times the length of the default setting

postconditions.name: time-limit-can-be-extended
postconditions.evidence: Extend The user is warned before time expires and given at least 20 seconds to extend the time limit with a simple action

postconditions.name: time-limit-required-for-real-time-event
postconditions.evidence: Real-time Exception The time limit is a required part of a real-time event (for example, an auction), and no alternative to the time limit is possible

postconditions.name: time-limit-essential-to-activity
postconditions.evidence: Essential Exception The time limit is essential and extending it would invalidate the activity

postconditions.name: time-limit-longer-than-twenty-hours
postconditions.evidence: 20 Hour Exception The time limit is longer than 20 hours

postconditions.name: moving-blinking-scrolling-can-be-paused-stopped-or-hidden
postconditions.evidence: there is a mechanism for the user to pause, stop, or hide it

postconditions.name: moving-blinking-scrolling-essential-to-activity
postconditions.evidence: unless the movement, blinking, or scrolling is part of an activity where it is essential

postconditions.name: auto-updating-can-be-paused-stopped-hidden-or-frequency-controlled
postconditions.evidence: there is a mechanism for the user to pause, stop, or hide it or to control the frequency of the update

postconditions.name: auto-updating-essential-to-activity
postconditions.evidence: unless the auto-updating is part of an activity where it is essential

postconditions.name: timing-not-essential-to-activity
postconditions.evidence: Timing is not an essential part of the event or activity presented by the content, except for non-interactive synchronized media and real-time events

postconditions.name: interruptions-can-be-postponed
postconditions.evidence: Interruptions can be postponed or suppressed by the user, except interruptions involving an emergency

postconditions.name: interruptions-can-be-suppressed
postconditions.evidence: Interruptions can be postponed or suppressed by the user, except interruptions involving an emergency

postconditions.name: activity-can-continue-without-data-loss-after-reauthentication
postconditions.evidence: the user can continue the activity without loss of data after re-authenticating

postconditions.name: inactivity-duration-causing-data-loss-warned-to-user
postconditions.evidence: Users are warned of the duration of any user inactivity that could cause data loss

postconditions.name: data-preserved-beyond-twenty-hours
postconditions.evidence: unless the data is preserved for more than 20 hours when the user does not take any actions

postconditions.name: no-more-than-three-flashes-in-any-one-second
postconditions.evidence: Web pages do not contain anything that flashes more than three times in any one second period

postconditions.name: flash-below-general-and-red-flash-thresholds
postconditions.evidence: or the flash is below the general flash and red flash thresholds

postconditions.name: content-does-not-flash-more-than-three-times-per-second
postconditions.evidence: Web pages do not contain anything that flashes more than three times in any one second period.

postconditions.name: interaction-triggered-motion-animation-can-be-disabled
postconditions.evidence: Motion animation triggered by interaction can be disabled, unless the animation is essential to the functionality or the information being conveyed.

postconditions.name: mechanism-to-bypass-repeated-blocks-available
postconditions.evidence: A mechanism is available to bypass blocks of content that are repeated on multiple web pages.

postconditions.name: title-present
postconditions.evidence: Web pages have titles

postconditions.name: title-describes-topic-or-purpose
postconditions.evidence: have titles that describe topic or purpose

postconditions.name: focus-order-preserves-meaning
postconditions.evidence: focusable components receive focus in an order that preserves meaning and operability

postconditions.name: focus-order-preserves-operability
postconditions.evidence: focusable components receive focus in an order that preserves meaning and operability

postconditions.name: link-purpose-determinable-from-link-text-alone
postconditions.evidence: The purpose of each link can be determined from the link text alone

postconditions.name: link-purpose-determinable-from-link-text-with-programmatic-context
postconditions.evidence: or from the link text together with its programmatically determined link context

postconditions.name: multiple-ways-to-locate-page-available
postconditions.evidence: More than one way is available to locate a web page within a set of web pages

postconditions.name: headings-describe-topic-or-purpose
postconditions.evidence: Headings and labels describe topic or purpose.

postconditions.name: labels-describe-topic-or-purpose
postconditions.evidence: Headings and labels describe topic or purpose.

postconditions.name: keyboard-focus-indicator-visible
postconditions.evidence: Any keyboard operable user interface has a mode of operation where the keyboard focus indicator is visible.

postconditions.name: location-within-page-set-information-available
postconditions.evidence: Information about the user's location within a set of web pages is available.

postconditions.name: mechanism-available-to-identify-link-purpose-from-link-text-alone
postconditions.evidence: A mechanism is available to allow the purpose of each link to be identified from link text alone

postconditions.name: section-headings-used-to-organize-content
postconditions.evidence: Section headings are used to organize the content.

postconditions.name: focused-component-not-entirely-hidden-by-author-content
postconditions.evidence: When a user interface component receives keyboard focus, the component is not entirely hidden due to author-created content.

postconditions.name: focused-component-no-part-hidden-by-author-content
postconditions.evidence: When a user interface component receives keyboard focus, no part of the component is hidden by author-created content.

postconditions.name: focus-indicator-area-at-least-2-css-pixel-perimeter
postconditions.evidence: is at least as large as the area of a 2 CSS pixel thick perimeter of the unfocused component or sub-component

postconditions.name: focus-indicator-contrast-ratio-at-least-3-to-1-between-focused-and-unfocused-states
postconditions.evidence: has a contrast ratio of at least 3:1 between the same pixels in the focused and unfocused states

postconditions.name: operable-with-single-pointer-without-path-based-gesture
postconditions.evidence: can be operated with a single pointer without a path-based gesture

verification_expression: down-event-not-used-to-execute-function OR (completion-on-up-event AND (mechanism-available-to-abort-before-completion OR mechanism-available-to-undo-after-completion)) OR up-event-reverses-down-event-outcome

postconditions.name: down-event-not-used-to-execute-function
postconditions.evidence: No Down-Event The down-event of the pointer is not used to execute any part of the function

postconditions.name: completion-on-up-event
postconditions.evidence: Abort or Undo Completion of the function is on the up-event

postconditions.name: mechanism-available-to-abort-before-completion
postconditions.evidence: a mechanism is available to abort the function before completion

postconditions.name: mechanism-available-to-undo-after-completion
postconditions.evidence: to undo the function after completion

postconditions.name: up-event-reverses-down-event-outcome
postconditions.evidence: Up Reversal The up-event reverses any outcome of the preceding down-event

postconditions.name: name-contains-visually-presented-label-text
postconditions.evidence: the name contains the text that is presented visually

postconditions.name: operable-by-user-interface-components
postconditions.evidence: can also be operated by user interface components

postconditions.name: motion-response-can-be-disabled
postconditions.evidence: responding to the motion can be disabled to prevent accidental actuation

postconditions.name: target-at-least-44-by-44-css-pixels
postconditions.evidence: The size of the target for pointer inputs is at least 44 by 44 CSS pixels

postconditions.name: equivalent-control-at-least-44-by-44-css-pixels-available
postconditions.evidence: Equivalent The target is available through an equivalent link or control on the same page that is at least 44 by 44 CSS pixels

postconditions.name: input-modalities-not-restricted
postconditions.evidence: Web content does not restrict use of input modalities available on a platform

postconditions.name: functionality-achievable-by-single-pointer-without-dragging
postconditions.evidence: All functionality that uses a dragging movement for operation can be achieved by a single pointer without dragging

verification_expression: target-size-at-least-24-by-24-css-pixels OR undersized-targets-spaced-so-24-css-pixel-circles-do-not-intersect OR function-achievable-through-different-conforming-control OR target-inline-in-text OR target-size-determined-by-user-agent OR particular-target-presentation-essential

postconditions.name: target-size-at-least-24-by-24-css-pixels
postconditions.evidence: The size of the target for pointer inputs is at least 24 by 24 CSS pixels

postconditions.name: undersized-targets-spaced-so-24-css-pixel-circles-do-not-intersect
postconditions.evidence: Undersized targets (those less than 24 by 24 CSS pixels) are positioned so that if a 24 CSS pixel diameter circle is centered on the bounding box of each, the circles do not intersect another target or the circle for another undersized target

postconditions.name: function-achievable-through-different-conforming-control
postconditions.evidence: The function can be achieved through a different control on the same page that meets this criterion

postconditions.name: target-inline-in-text
postconditions.evidence: The target is in a sentence or its size is otherwise constrained by the line-height of non-target text

postconditions.name: target-size-determined-by-user-agent
postconditions.evidence: The size of the target is determined by the user agent and is not modified by the author

postconditions.name: particular-target-presentation-essential
postconditions.evidence: A particular presentation of the target is essential or is legally required for the information being conveyed

postconditions.name: default-language-programmatically-determinable
postconditions.evidence: The default human language of each web page can be programmatically determined

postconditions.name: passage-or-phrase-language-programmatically-determinable
postconditions.evidence: The human language of each passage or phrase in the content can be programmatically determined

postconditions.name: mechanism-available-to-identify-definitions
postconditions.evidence: A mechanism is available for identifying specific definitions of words or phrases used in an unusual or restricted way, including idioms and jargon

postconditions.name: mechanism-available-to-identify-expanded-form-or-meaning
postconditions.evidence: A mechanism for identifying the expanded form or meaning of abbreviations is available

postconditions.name: supplemental-content-available
postconditions.evidence: supplemental content, or a version that does not require reading ability more advanced than the lower secondary education level, is available

postconditions.name: simplified-version-not-requiring-advanced-reading-available
postconditions.evidence: a version that does not require reading ability more advanced than the lower secondary education level, is available

postconditions.name: mechanism-available-to-identify-pronunciation
postconditions.evidence: A mechanism is available for identifying specific pronunciation of words where meaning of the words, in context, is ambiguous without knowing the pronunciation

postconditions.name: focus-does-not-initiate-change-of-context
postconditions.evidence: When any user interface component receives focus, it does not initiate a change of context

postconditions.name: setting-change-does-not-cause-change-of-context
postconditions.evidence: Changing the setting of any user interface component does not automatically cause a change of context

postconditions.name: user-advised-of-behavior-before-use
postconditions.evidence: unless the user has been advised of the behavior before using the component

postconditions.name: repeated-navigation-occurs-in-same-relative-order
postconditions.evidence: Navigational mechanisms that are repeated on multiple web pages within a set of web pages occur in the same relative order each time they are repeated

postconditions.name: change-initiated-by-user
postconditions.evidence: unless a change is initiated by the user

postconditions.name: same-functionality-components-identified-consistently
postconditions.evidence: Components that have the same functionality within a set of web pages are identified consistently

postconditions.name: changes-of-context-initiated-only-by-user-request
postconditions.evidence: Changes of context are initiated only by user request

postconditions.name: mechanism-to-turn-off-changes-available
postconditions.evidence: or a mechanism is available to turn off such changes

postconditions.name: repeated-help-mechanisms-occur-in-same-relative-order
postconditions.evidence: those mechanisms are repeated on multiple web pages within a set of web pages, they occur in the same order relative to other page content

postconditions.name: change-initiated-by-user
postconditions.evidence: unless a change is initiated by the user

postconditions.name: erroneous-item-identified
postconditions.evidence: the item that is in error is identified

postconditions.name: error-described-to-user-in-text
postconditions.evidence: the error is described to the user in text

postconditions.name: labels-provided
postconditions.evidence: Labels or instructions are provided when content requires user input

postconditions.name: instructions-provided
postconditions.evidence: Labels or instructions are provided when content requires user input

postconditions.name: correction-suggestions-provided
postconditions.evidence: the suggestions are provided to the user

postconditions.name: providing-suggestions-would-jeopardize-security-or-purpose
postconditions.evidence: unless it would jeopardize the security or purpose of the content

postconditions.name: submissions-reversible
postconditions.evidence: ReversibleSubmissions are reversible

postconditions.name: data-checked-for-input-errors
postconditions.evidence: CheckedData entered by the user is checked for input errors

postconditions.name: correction-opportunity-provided
postconditions.evidence: the user is provided an opportunity to correct them

postconditions.name: mechanism-to-review-confirm-correct-before-finalizing
postconditions.evidence: ConfirmedA mechanism is available for reviewing, confirming, and correcting information before finalizing the submission

postconditions.name: context-sensitive-help-available
postconditions.evidence: Context-sensitive help is available

postconditions.name: submissions-reversible
postconditions.evidence: Submissions are reversible.

postconditions.name: data-checked-for-input-errors
postconditions.evidence: Data entered by the user is checked for input errors

postconditions.name: opportunity-to-correct-provided
postconditions.evidence: the user is provided an opportunity to correct them

postconditions.name: mechanism-for-review-confirm-correct-before-finalizing
postconditions.evidence: A mechanism is available for reviewing, confirming, and correcting information before finalizing the submission.

postconditions.name: information-auto-populated
postconditions.evidence: is either: auto-populated

postconditions.name: information-available-to-select
postconditions.evidence: available for the user to select

verification_expression: cognitive-function-test-not-required OR alternative-authentication-method-available OR mechanism-to-assist-completing-test-available OR test-is-object-recognition OR test-is-personal-content-identification

postconditions.name: cognitive-function-test-not-required
postconditions.evidence: A cognitive function test (such as remembering a password or solving a puzzle) is not required for any step in an authentication process

postconditions.name: alternative-authentication-method-available
postconditions.evidence: Another authentication method that does not rely on a cognitive function test.

postconditions.name: mechanism-to-assist-completing-test-available
postconditions.evidence: A mechanism is available to assist the user in completing the cognitive function test.

postconditions.name: test-is-object-recognition
postconditions.evidence: The cognitive function test is to recognize objects.

postconditions.name: test-is-personal-content-identification
postconditions.evidence: The cognitive function test is to identify non-text content the user provided to the website.

postconditions.name: cognitive-function-test-not-required
postconditions.evidence: A cognitive function test (such as remembering a password or solving a puzzle) is not required for any step in an authentication process

postconditions.name: alternative-authentication-method-available
postconditions.evidence: Another authentication method that does not rely on a cognitive function test.

postconditions.name: mechanism-to-assist-completing-test-available
postconditions.evidence: A mechanism is available to assist the user in completing the cognitive function test.

verification_expression: name-programmatically-determinable AND role-programmatically-determinable AND user-settable-states-properties-values-programmatically-settable AND changes-notified-to-user-agents

postconditions.name: name-programmatically-determinable
postconditions.evidence: the name and role can be programmatically determined

postconditions.name: role-programmatically-determinable
postconditions.evidence: the name and role can be programmatically determined

postconditions.name: user-settable-states-properties-values-programmatically-settable
postconditions.evidence: states, properties, and values that can be set by the user can be programmatically set

postconditions.name: changes-notified-to-user-agents
postconditions.evidence: notification of changes to these items is available to user agents, including assistive technologies

postconditions.name: status-messages-programmatically-determinable-through-role-or-properties
postconditions.evidence: status messages can be programmatically determined through role or properties

postconditions.name: presentable-by-assistive-technologies-without-receiving-focus
postconditions.evidence: they can be presented to the user by assistive technologies without receiving focus
